description_long: This is a weathered, cylindrical fire hydrant with a domed top and three prominent hose connection outlets, two on the sides and one facing forward. The hydrant is predominantly blue with significant patches of rust and wear, giving it a rugged, aged appearance. Chains are attached to the caps of the outlets, likely to prevent loss or theft, and hang loosely against the body. The surface is ribbed vertically, adding texture and structural strength, and the base is wider, sitting atop a patch of worn grass and dirt. The top features a central operating nut or cap, used for opening the hydrant. No visible manufacturer or model markings are present on the exterior.
description: A weathered blue fire hydrant with rust patches, three hose outlets, and attached chains, sitting on a patch of grass and dirt.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main hose outlet with a cap and chain, and the domed top with the operating nut.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one of the side outlets with its cap and chain, and the ribbed cylindrical body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying both side outlets and the back of the hydrant, with visible rust and surface texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other side outlet and the back of the main outlet, with chains and cap details.
